raw_input:
[1.0/5] VERY UNSATISFIED, PISSED ACTUALLY Reviews were good, it was my favorite color, couldn't wait to try when I received it. When I plugged it in and attempted to use, the sucker wouldn't come on. Defected. Will purchase item from different manufacturer. [3.0/5] soft results! I absolutley love how this makes my hair feel. I have naturally wavy hair, but sometimes its a pain with frizz. I was a bit disappointed there are no instructions. It also has the control buttons right where your thumb grips the handle, which isnt the best. Supposedly you can control the temperature, but I had no luck with this at all. So if you want to plug it in and go, its perfect. But if you want to control it, its not for you. Im still in love with the color, the box is great for storing, and it doesnt burn you when brushing. It took me two minutes to brush out my hair. Also dont do it wet, because it pulls horridly. But to wake up and go its great. I recieved this product at a discount for my honest opinion. If my review helped please like my post [5.0/5] Straightens without flattening hair I had been wanting a straightening brush for quite a while. Most of them had really mixed reviews, so I was hesitant to buy one. I jumped at the opportunity to buy this one at a discount to review. I decided on this one because it was advertised as professional quality, actually looked like a salon quality product that would last, and because it is purple. When I received the product, I could tell I made a great choice.

The controls are on the side. I was skeptical about that because many other products had reviews which said that the buttons were on the side and it was easy to accidentally turn it off or change the temperature. I did not find this to be true with this brush. To change the temperature or turn off the brush, I had to press and hold the buttons in a way that would be difficult to do inadvertently. I initially set the temperature at 400, and then decided to increase to 450 when I got to a stubborn section of my hair. I kept checking the temperature reading as I used the Envy brush, and it did not budge until I purposely changed the setting.

This brush heated up quickly. It gets really hot, but I thought it was easy to use safely. It seems like it would be difficult to accidentally burn yourself, although not impossible.

My hair is long and somewhat fine in texture, but I have a lot of hair. It usually takes me 20-30 minutes to straighten my hair with a CHI professional flat iron. I initially just started to use the Envy brush right after blow drying my hair. After a few brushes, I realized that I would need to completely comb through my hair to smooth it out before using this brush. I started out by just running the Envy brush through my hair. I found quickly that I needed to section off my hair for the best results. I sectioned off the top 2/3 of my hair, twisted and secured it to the top of my head, and focused on the underside first. When I finished the under layer, I moved on to the middle section, and then the top. The whole process took me about 20-25 minutes. This is close to the same amount of time it takes me to use a flat iron. I think the Envy brush will go much faster the next time I use it. There is a bit of a learning curve, but I felt comfortable with the technique by the time I was done styling my hair.

One difference I noticed was the way my hair felt after styling with the Envy brush. When I use my flat iron, my hair is...flat. The flat iron leaves my hair very smooth but it doesn't seem to have much texture or body left after straightening. When I used the Envy brush, my hair was straight, but still had plenty of volume.

I'm really happy I bought the Envy styling brush. I really like the volume my hair has after using this brush. I highly recommend this styling brush! [4.0/5] I have coily curly African hair which I stretch using ... I have coily curly African hair which I stretch using a roller set. I bought this brush to straighten my hair further without using a flat iron or blow dryer. It does the job! If you have curls and you want a bone straight look, this might not be the right tool for you. But, if you want to straighten it enough to maximize the length of your style without too much heat damage and effort, this is it! [5.0/5] LOVE LOVE LOVE!! LOVE LOVE LOVE!
I have always wanted to get one of these fancy hair straightening brushes but just never got around to it until I saw this one online and decided to finally just purchase it. I am so glad I did! This brush is amazing! It is not only very easy to use, but works wonders. I will say that I do still love my straighter a little more because it does not pull my thin blonde hair as much, but it is well worth the money!
I tired this brush on my sister who has very curly light brown hair and she was amazed at how well, and fast it straightened her hair. When it normally took her an hour to straighten her hair with my straighten only took her 15 minutes with this brush!
The one down side to the brush is that the heat protectors on the hot bristle head are somewhat jell like. If you do not let the brush fully heat up to the temperature you would like to use, I have noticed that the brush will pull on more of your hair, possibly ripping it out.
The color of this brush, a very pretty purple, and the black cord makes the brush look very high class. The brush looks and works like a brush that should cost much more than $50. Heck my straighter was was twice that much and is nothing compared to this hair straightening brush with my sisters hair.
All in all, if you have thin, blonde hair like myself, this brush is nice to have but huge need to upgrade your flat iron straightener. BUT if you have super curly, thick hair, you should really think about getting this brush! You will not regret it!
I received this product at a discounted rate or free in exchange for my honest and unbiased review. [3.0/5] Not Great for Thick/Wavy Hair. Pretty & OK for Touch-Ups My whole life I've needed to use a hair straightener. I have very thick strands of hair and a lot of it. Plus, my hair is poofy crazy curly. My hair used to be down to my butt, however, this past year I had to cut it all off due to getting a horrid matted mess. (If you don't know what it means to have matted hair - your lucky). It was a whole bunch of tears, pain, tantrums, home remedies, a ton of hair salon visits and a ton of money spent until realizing that I was s*** outta luck and had to cry as they cut my hair to only an inch in the back, but luckily had enough hair left / saved on top and on sides for shoulder length hair. So now more than ever, having short hair that's very thick and curly, I'm always looking for amazing hair straighteners and products in general.

I have wanted one of these heat and style brushes and finally decided to test this one out. I loved the color of this heat brush and it looked like a nice, high quality hair straightening brush in the images.

This brush has less options then I thought it would. I cannot control the temperature and I would like for it to get a little bit hotter. Since I have curly, wavy hair this doesn't heat up enough to get it straight. Or maybe it's not doing a great job because it doesn't have two "plates" like regular straighteners. If I straighten my hair with a flat iron, this is a great touch up tool for the days following the flat iron before I wash my hair again. SO - It does work, but it is NOT a miracle product. This hair straightening brush (& all brush type of straighteners) MIGHT work better / work faster / have better results if you have thin hair? Just a assumption.

One difference I noticed was the way my hair felt after styling with the Envy brush. When I use my flat iron, my hair is...flat. The flat iron leaves my hair very smooth but it doesn't seem to have much texture or body left after straightening. When I used the Envy brush, my hair was straight, but still had plenty of volume. I'm really happy I bought the Envy styling brush. I really like the volume my hair has after using this brush.

I had to completely comb through my hair to smooth it out before using this brush (obviously). I started out by just running the Envy brush through my hair. Once I was done brushing my hair, I sectioned off my hair for the best results. I sectioned off the top 2/3 of my hair, twisted and secured it to the top of my head. As always, I focused on the underside first. When I finished the under layer, I moved on to the middle section, and then the top. The whole process took me about 20minutes. Pretty close to the same amount of time that it takes me to use my regular flat iron.

An ion is as an atom in which the number of electrons and protons are not the same. When a hair tool has ionic in its name, it means that it can generate negative ions. Hair has high water content, making it positively charged. When negative ions come in contact with hair, the positive charge is neutralized. This leaves your hair straight, sleek, shiny and smooth.

Here is one crucial thing to keep in mind – not all flat irons are pure ceramic, many of them are aluminum. They are just coated with ceramic on the top. Such models are less expensive, but you need to be extra careful when using them. This coating can chip or peel, revealing less quality metal below. These peeled areas can snag your hair! So always try to find out if an iron is coated or not.
You can even find costly professional models that are not 100% ceramic. Solid or pure ceramic are words that you are looking for.

I'm not sure if this brush is ceramic, titanium or what..?!?

Titanium vs. Ceramic
In this battle, it is obvious that titanium is a winner. But does this mean that you should use only this type of iron? No. Many experts say that consumers should use ceramic for one obvious reason. Since titanium can get very hot and transfers heat better, it’s very easy to damage your hair if you are not careful. Also, this high heat is only advantageous for women with thick, coarse and stubborn hair. If your have thin or fragile hair, there is no need for high temperatures. In the end, it is up to you to find out what will work best for your hair. Now you have all the facts to make the right decision. If you are still indecisive, there is a simple solution! Get a flat iron that combines ceramic and titanium, such as Croc 2 Infrared mentioned above. This way you get ceramic’s safety and protection and titanium’s speed and efficiency.

I received this product at a discount or free in exchange for an honest review. I strongly depend on product reviews when making purchases so it's very important for me to leave an honest review. I do NOT review products just because they are free or discounted. That said, I only accept promotional items that I would actually purchase for myself and my family. The discount in no way affects my opinion, rating, or review of a product in question.
  I have read the reviews and information about the product myself prior to ordering so I am usually confident I will like the product – thus the reason many of my reviews are positive. However, I have and will continue to leave appropriate negative reviews for products that are not good quality products.
  Please note I have no relationship with this seller and I’m not compensated in any way for my review. [3.0/5] Not too bad I've used this twice and every time it's pulling my hair out. I have very fine hair and I feel it gets tangled easily. Other than that, I love the way it makes my hair look and feel. [1.0/5] Worst product I have EVER purchased Worst product I have EVER purchased. It dried my hair terribly, I used it 2 weeks ago and my hair is still super dry, I’ve spent over $60 on hair products trying to bring it back to life and nothing will work, especially my ends. [1.0/5] This product is useless. when I bought it was very expensive This product is useless .when I bought it was very expensive....I wish I can return it back
let me know if there is any compensation for that useless product [3.0/5] I loved the color of this heat brush and it looked ... I loved the color of this heat brush and it looked like it was high quality in the images. I have wanted one of these heat and style brushes and finally decided to test this one out. This brush has less options then I thought it would. I cannot control the temperature and I would like for it to get a little bit hotter. I have fairly wavy hair and this doesn't heat up enough to get it straight. However, if I straighten my hair with a flat iron this is a great touch up tool for the days following the flat iron before I was my hair again. It does work, but it is not a miracle product.

I did receive this product at a discount in exchange for my honest and unbiased opinion. All comments and thoughts expressed are my own, based upon my experiences with the product.

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

The reviews are mixed overall. Some customers love the brush and find it works great, while others are disappointed or had bad experiences.

On the positive side, several reviewers praised the brush for straightening hair effectively while leaving volume and body, unlike a flat iron which leaves hair flat. One reviewer with fine blonde hair found it easy to use and worth the money, and was impressed that it straightened her sister's curly hair in 15 minutes instead of an hour. Another reviewer with fine hair found it comparable in time to a flat iron but preferred the results. The purple color and overall appearance were frequently praised as looking high quality and attractive.

On the negative side, some customers received defective units that did not work at all. One reviewer reported the brush dried out her hair badly, leaving it super dry and requiring 60 dollars in hair products to repair. Another found it pulled and tangled fine hair. Some felt the temperature control was limited and wished it could get hotter, particularly for thick or coily hair. A few felt the product was overpriced for what it delivered.

Several reviewers noted a learning curve, recommending sectioning hair and combing it smooth before using the brush for best results. Most agreed the brush works better on thin to medium hair than on very thick, coarse, or tightly coiled hair.
